subject: Mathematics
title: Gauss: The Prince of Mathematicians
description: One-minute skill: Gauss: The Prince of Mathematicians.

hook.text: At age seven, Carl Gauss added the numbers 1 to 100 in seconds—while his classmates struggled for an hour.

buildup.text: He noticed that pairing first and last terms gives 101, fifty times: 1+100, 2+99, 3+98, all equal 101.

discovery.text: By 21, he had proven the fundamental theorem of algebra and published a masterwork on number theory.

twist.text: He kept many discoveries private, publishing only polished results. Some were found only after his death.

climax.text: He contributed to statistics, magnetism, optics, and astronomy—earning the title Prince of Mathematicians.

punchline.text: A seven-year-old's shortcut revealed a lifelong genius.

quiz.question: How did young Gauss quickly sum the numbers 1 to 100?
options: By pairing numbers that sum to 101
